tasks.title: Verify branch before edits
tasks.description: Confirm working on a dedicated feature branch for the Buy Next refinement before any code changes.
tasks.details:
Run `git branch --show-current`. Expected output: a branch like `feature/buy-next-refine` (NOT main). If on main, run:

    git checkout -b feature/buy-next-refine

If the original Buy Next work is on `feature/buy-next-page` and unmerged, branch off it instead so this refactor is reviewable as its own diff:

    git checkout feature/buy-next-page
    git checkout -b feature/buy-next-refine

Then `git status` must be clean. Do not edit any source files until both checks pass.
tasks.testStrategy: `git branch --show-current` prints the dedicated feature branch name. `git status` shows a clean working tree.

tasks.title: Refactor /api/buy-next: flat rows, three metrics, my_routes join
tasks.description: Replace the per-aircraft recommendation logic with a flat (route × aircraft × config) query that returns all three profitability metrics, joins my_routes for highlight tiers, and supports five sort options.
tasks.details:
Edit dashboard/routes/api_routes.py. Replace the existing api_buy_next handler.

New signature:

    @router.get("/buy-next", response_class=HTMLResponse)
    def api_buy_next(
        request: Request,
        hub: str = Query(""),
        budget: Optional[str] = Query(None),
        sort: str = Query("price_desc"),
        ac_type: str = Query(""),
        exclude_owned: int = Query(0),
        hide_stopovers: int = Query(0),
        hide_existing: int = Query(0),
        limit: int = Query(15, ge=1, le=500),
    ):
        ...

Validation:
  - hub REQUIRED. Empty → render 'Pick a hub and enter a budget' prompt.
  - budget REQUIRED. Parse Optional[str]: empty → prompt; non-numeric → 'Enter a valid budget'; negative → invalid; '100000000.50' → try float() then int(); '0' → valid but yields empty result.
  - sort whitelist: {'price_desc', 'price_asc', 'profit_desc', 'profit_asc', 'yield_desc', 'total_desc'}. Default 'price_desc'. Anything else → silently fall back to default.
  - ac_type whitelist: {'', 'pax', 'cargo', 'vip'}.

Main SQL — flat rows with LEFT JOIN to my_routes for highlight tiering:

    SELECT a_dest.iata AS destination,
           a_dest.country AS dest_country,
           a_dest.id AS dest_id,
           ac.id AS aircraft_id,
           ac.shortname AS ac_shortname,
           ac.name AS ac_name,
           ac.type AS ac_type,
           ac.cost AS ac_cost,
           ac.capacity AS ac_capacity,
           ac.range_km AS ac_range,
           ra.distance_km,
           ra.config_y, ra.config_j, ra.config_f,
           ra.profit_per_trip,
           ra.trips_per_day,
           ra.profit_per_ac_day,
           ra.flight_time_hrs,
           ra.needs_stopover,
           ra.stopover_iata,
           CASE WHEN ac.cost > 0
                THEN (ra.profit_per_ac_day * 1000000.0 / ac.cost)
                ELSE 0
           END AS profit_yield,
           mr.id AS my_route_id,
           mr.aircraft_id AS my_route_ac_id,
           current_ac.shortname AS current_ac_shortname,
           current_ac.name AS current_ac_name,
           mr.num_assigned AS current_num_assigned
    FROM route_aircraft ra
    JOIN aircraft ac ON ra.aircraft_id = ac.id
    JOIN airports a_dest ON ra.dest_id = a_dest.id
    LEFT JOIN my_routes mr ON mr.origin_id = ra.origin_id AND mr.dest_id = ra.dest_id
    LEFT JOIN aircraft current_ac ON mr.aircraft_id = current_ac.id
    WHERE ra.is_valid = 1
      AND ra.origin_id = ?
      AND ac.cost > 0
      AND ac.cost <= ?
      AND ra.profit_per_ac_day > 0
      [AND ac.type = ?]                                       -- if ac_type set
      [AND ra.needs_stopover = 0]                             -- if hide_stopovers
      [AND ac.id NOT IN (SELECT aircraft_id FROM my_fleet)]   -- if exclude_owned
      [AND mr.id IS NULL]                                     -- if hide_existing
    ORDER BY <sort_clause>
    LIMIT ?

IMPORTANT: the LEFT JOIN to my_routes can produce duplicate rows if a single (origin, dest) somehow has multiple my_routes entries (the schema's UNIQUE constraint on (origin_id, dest_id, aircraft_id) allows multiple aircraft on the same route). Wrap the my_routes lookup in a subquery to collapse to one row per (origin, dest), preferring the entry with the highest num_assigned. Use a CTE:

    WITH my_routes_collapsed AS (
        SELECT origin_id, dest_id,
               MIN(id) AS id,
               MAX(aircraft_id) AS aircraft_id,
               SUM(num_assigned) AS num_assigned
        FROM my_routes
        GROUP BY origin_id, dest_id
    )
    SELECT ... FROM route_aircraft ra ... LEFT JOIN my_routes_collapsed mr ON ...

This means the 'currently' display shows aggregated info — if you fly DXB→LHR with 3 different aircraft, the column will show one of them with the total num_assigned. Document this in a comment in the SQL.

To also detect EXACT matches (same hub + dest + aircraft) for the checkmark tier, add a separate computed flag in the SELECT:

    EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM my_routes mr_exact
        WHERE mr_exact.origin_id = ra.origin_id
          AND mr_exact.dest_id   = ra.dest_id
          AND mr_exact.aircraft_id = ra.aircraft_id
    ) AS is_exact_match

Sort clause map (note new total_desc; deterministic secondary keys for every primary):

    SORT_CLAUSES = {
        'price_desc':  'ac.cost DESC, ra.profit_per_ac_day DESC',
        'price_asc':   'ac.cost ASC,  ra.profit_per_ac_day DESC',
        'profit_desc': 'ra.profit_per_ac_day DESC, ac.cost DESC',
        'profit_asc':  'ra.profit_per_ac_day ASC,  ac.cost ASC',
        'yield_desc':  'profit_yield DESC, ra.profit_per_ac_day DESC',
        'total_desc':  '(? / ac.cost) * ra.profit_per_ac_day DESC, ra.profit_per_ac_day DESC',
    }

For 'total_desc' the budget must be passed as the first param of the ORDER BY expression. Easiest approach: don't use bind params in ORDER BY (some SQLite versions choke). Instead compute total_daily_profit in Python and sort the result list there for this one case. Pseudo:

    rows = fetch_all(...)
    for r in rows:
        r['qty_affordable']     = budget_val // r['ac_cost']
        r['total_daily_profit'] = r['qty_affordable'] * r['profit_per_ac_day']
        r['payback_days']       = round(r['ac_cost'] / r['profit_per_ac_day'], 1) if r['profit_per_ac_day'] > 0 else None
    if sort == 'total_desc':
        rows.sort(key=lambda r: (r['total_daily_profit'], r['profit_per_ac_day']), reverse=True)

Note: when sorting in Python, we lose the SQL LIMIT optimization. To compensate, fetch (limit * 5) rows from SQL with a sane SQL ordering (e.g. profit_per_ac_day DESC) and then re-sort and trim in Python. This keeps the result reasonably close to globally optimal without scanning the entire table.

After sorting, identify the BEST BUY for the 🏆 badge — the row with the maximum total_daily_profit across the visible result set:

    if rows:
        best_buy_idx = max(range(len(rows)), key=lambda i: rows[i]['total_daily_profit'])
        rows[best_buy_idx]['is_best_buy'] = True

Classify each row's match tier (used by the partial for highlight color):

    for r in rows:
        if r.get('is_exact_match'):
            r['match_tier'] = 'exact'      # checkmark, dim
        elif r.get('my_route_id'):
            r['match_tier'] = 'route'      # blue tint
        else:
            r['match_tier'] = 'none'

Detect whether the result set hit the limit (for the 'Show all' footnote in the partial):

    truncated = (len(rows) >= limit)

Pass rows + sort + budget_val + hub + truncated + limit to the partial. The 'Show all' button in the partial will re-fire with limit=500.
tasks.testStrategy:
1) GET /api/buy-next?hub=DXB&budget=200000000 → 200, ≤15 rows, sorted by ac.cost DESC.
2) Sort=total_desc → first row has the maximum (qty_affordable × profit_per_ac_day) in the visible set.
3) Sort=yield_desc → first row has the highest profit_yield.
4) Pick a route in my_routes with a different aircraft than what Buy Next suggests → that row has match_tier='route', current_ac_shortname populated.
5) Pick a route in my_routes with the SAME aircraft → match_tier='exact'.
6) hide_existing=1 → no rows where my_route_id is set.
7) The row with the highest total_daily_profit across the visible set has is_best_buy=True regardless of current sort.
8) When 15+ rows match the filters, truncated=True is passed to the template.
9) GET /api/buy-next?hub=DXB&budget=200000000&sort=invalid → falls back to price_desc, no error.
10) GET /api/buy-next?hub=DXB&budget=abc → 'Enter a valid budget' message, no 500.

tasks.title: Update buy_next.html: required inputs, sort dropdown, hide-existing toggle
tasks.description: Update the page template so hub and budget are required, add the sort dropdown with 6 options including profit yield and total/day, and add the hide-existing-routes toggle.
tasks.details:
Edit dashboard/templates/buy_next.html. Replace the existing form. Key changes from prior version: - Hub dropdown: REMOVE 'All hubs'. First option: ``. Add a small link beside the form: `Browse globally instead →`. - Budget input: `required` attribute, `placeholder="e.g. 100000000"`, no default value. - NEW sort dropdown (6 options, default = price_desc):
- NEW checkbox: hide_existing
- Carry forward existing controls: ac_type, exclude_owned, hide_stopovers (add hide_stopovers if missing). - Results div: NO hx-trigger="load". Initial state shows 'Pick a hub and enter a budget' prompt. Update the page subheading: 'Pick a hub and budget. See every route × aircraft × seat config you can afford. The 🏆 row is the highest total daily profit you could earn by spending your full budget on it. Routes you already operate are highlighted in blue (✓ if same aircraft).'
tasks.testStrategy:
1) /buy-next initial load: empty results pane, no network call.
2) Sort dropdown shows 6 options.
3) Hide-existing checkbox present and re-fires on toggle.
4) Budget input is browser-required (HTML5 validation blocks empty submission).
5) 'Browse globally instead →' link visible and points to /buy-next/global.

tasks.title: Rewrite partials/buy_next_results.html: 15 rows, highlights, 🏆 badge, Show All
tasks.description: Replace the per-aircraft card layout with a flat table limited to 15 rows. Includes two-tier row highlighting for existing routes, a 🏆 badge on the best-buy row, clickable column headers, and a 'Show all' button when truncated.
tasks.details:
Edit dashboard/templates/partials/buy_next_results.html. Drop the card structure entirely. Structure: {% if not rows %}

No affordable routes match these filters. Try raising the budget or clearing the type filter.

{% else %}
Showing {{ rows|length }} rows for hub {{ hub }} under ${{ "{:,}".format(budget) }} · sort: {{ sort }}
{% for r in rows %} {% endfor %}
Dest Aircraft Type A/C $ Y/J/F Dist Profit/d Yield Qty Total/d Payback Currently
{% if r.is_best_buy %}🏆{% endif %} {{ r.destination }} {{ r.dest_country }} {% if r.needs_stopover %} ⚠{% endif %} {{ r.ac_name }} {{ r.ac_shortname }} {{ r.ac_type }} ${{ "{:,}".format(r.ac_cost) }} {{ r.config_y }}/{{ r.config_j }}/{{ r.config_f }} {{ "{:,.0f}".format(r.distance_km or 0) }} ${{ "{:,.0f}".format(r.profit_per_ac_day or 0) }} ${{ "{:,.0f}".format(r.profit_yield or 0) }} {{ r.qty_affordable }} ${{ "{:,.0f}".format(r.total_daily_profit or 0) }} {% if r.payback_days %}{{ r.payback_days }}d{% else %}—{% endif %} {% if r.match_tier == 'exact' %} ✓ same {% elif r.match_tier == 'route' %} {{ r.current_ac_shortname }} {% endif %}
{% if truncated %}
Showing first {{ limit }} rows. More matches exist.
{% endif %} {% endif %} Legend strip below the table:
Route you already operate (different aircraft) ✓ same Exact route + aircraft you already operate 🏆 Highest total daily profit at your budget ⚠ Needs stopover
Note on hx-vals + hx-include: when both define a `sort` value, hx-vals takes precedence. Confirmed by HTMX docs. Same applies to the limit override on the 'Show all' button.
tasks.testStrategy:
1) Render with rows → 15 max, 🏆 on the highest total/day row.
2) Render with row count == limit → 'Show all matches' button appears.
3) Click 'Show all' → re-fires with limit=500, button disappears.
4) Render a row with match_tier='route' → blue background, current_ac_shortname in 'Currently' column.
5) Render a row with match_tier='exact' → dim background, ✓ same in 'Currently' column.
6) Click 'Total/d' header → re-sorts by total_desc. 🏆 should still be on the same row (it tracks max regardless of sort).
7) Empty result → friendly message, no table, no legend.
8) Legend strip visible below populated table.

tasks.title: Create /buy-next/global page for cross-hub browsing
tasks.description: Separate page that runs the same query without a hub filter, showing routes across all hubs. Adds a Hub column and uses a tighter default limit due to scale.
tasks.details: This is the answer to '#9 — what about browsing globally without a hub?' It's a separate page so the main /buy-next stays focused on the hub+budget decision. Files to create: - dashboard/templates/buy_next_global.html - dashboard/templates/partials/buy_next_global_results.html Files to edit: - dashboard/routes/pages.py (add page route) - dashboard/routes/api_routes.py (add /api/buy-next-global endpoint) - dashboard/templates/base.html (no nav change — link only from /buy-next page) Page route in pages.py: @router.get("/buy-next/global", response_class=HTMLResponse) def page_buy_next_global(request: Request): ctx = base_context(request) return templates.TemplateResponse(request, "buy_next_global.html", ctx) No hub list needed in context — there's no hub picker. API endpoint /api/buy-next-global: @router.get("/buy-next-global", response_class=HTMLResponse) def api_buy_next_global( request: Request, budget: Optional[str] = Query(None), sort: str = Query("total_desc"), # default total_desc — globally we want best opportunity ac_type: str = Query(""), exclude_owned: int = Query(0), hide_stopovers: int = Query(0), hide_existing: int = Query(0), limit: int = Query(15, ge=1, le=100), # tighter cap than per-hub ): ... Key differences from /api/buy-next: - No hub param. WHERE clause drops `ra.origin_id = ?`. - SELECT adds `a_orig.iata AS hub` and joins airports as a_orig on ra.origin_id = a_orig.id. - LEFT JOIN to my_routes_collapsed stays the same. - Default sort = 'total_desc' — globally, the most useful default is 'where can I deploy my whole budget for the highest total profit', not 'most expensive aircraft'. - Max limit 100 (vs 500 on the per-hub endpoint) because the underlying result set is potentially 100k+ rows. - Pre-fetch budget × 5 = 75 rows from SQL with a sane SQL ORDER BY (profit_per_ac_day DESC), then re-sort and trim in Python — same approach as task 2 for total_desc. Template buy_next_global.html: same structure as buy_next.html but without the hub dropdown, with the heading 'Buy Next — Global Browse', and a back-link `← Per-hub view`. Default sort dropdown selection = total_desc. Partial buy_next_global_results.html: same as buy_next_results.html but with an extra leftmost data column 'Hub' showing the origin IATA, and uses /api/buy-next-global in all hx-get URLs. WARNING about scale: with 6 hubs × ~150 destinations × ~80 affordable aircraft ≈ 72,000 rows pre-LIMIT. Make sure the SQL is using the existing indexes (idx_ra_valid_profit, idx_ra_origin_ac). Add EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN to the SQL during development to verify. If performance is bad, consider pre-filtering to top routes per hub via a window function CTE before the main SELECT.
tasks.testStrategy:
1) GET /buy-next/global → 200, page renders with budget input and sort dropdown, no hub picker.
2) Enter budget = 200000000 → results table populates, sorted by total_desc by default.
3) Hub column is visible and shows mixed hubs across rows.
4) Same row highlighting and 🏆 logic as the per-hub page.
5) Page response time stays under 2s for budget=200M; if not, profile the SQL.
6) Back-link returns to /buy-next.

tasks.title: Edge case hardening for both endpoints
tasks.description: Ensure /api/buy-next and /api/buy-next-global handle malformed inputs, empty results, and zero-cost edge cases without 500s.
tasks.details:
Verify and harden the following in BOTH endpoints (api_buy_next and api_buy_next_global):

  1. budget = '' → 'Pick a hub and enter a budget' (or 'Enter a budget' for the global page).
  2. budget = '0' → empty result, friendly 'No matches' message.
  3. budget = '-100' → 'Enter a valid budget'.
  4. budget = '100000000.50' → try float() then int(). Should work and treat as 100000000.
  5. budget = 'abc' → 'Enter a valid budget'.
  6. sort outside whitelist → fall back to default silently.
  7. limit > max → Query already enforces le=500/100 via FastAPI; verify by passing limit=99999 → should return 422 with a clean error, not crash.
  8. ac.cost = 0 (data quality) → filtered by `ac.cost > 0` in WHERE.
  9. profit_per_ac_day = 0 or NULL → filtered by `ra.profit_per_ac_day > 0`.
  10. Division-by-zero in profit_yield → guarded by CASE WHEN ac.cost > 0.
  11. my_routes_collapsed CTE returns no rows for the hub → all rows have match_tier='none'. Verified.
  12. is_best_buy on empty rows list → guarded by `if rows:` before computing max.
  13. DB file missing → wrap get_db() in try/except FileNotFoundError, return amber notice.
  14. /api/buy-next-global with no params → since hub isn't required, just budget validation kicks in.

Also add a unit-test-style script in /tmp during development that hits each endpoint with each edge-case URL via curl and inspects the response code + body. Don't commit the script.
tasks.testStrategy: Run curl against all 14 cases for both endpoints. None return 500. Each returns the appropriate friendly message or row list. The 'Show all matches' footnote appears only when len(rows) == limit.

tasks.title: Smoke test in browser
tasks.description: Walk the full user flow on both pages and verify all features behave as specified.
tasks.details:
Start the dashboard:

    python main.py dashboard

Per-hub /buy-next walkthrough:
  1. Open /buy-next: empty pane, prompt visible, no network call.
  2. Hub = DXB, budget = 200000000. Results populate, sorted by A/C price desc, ≤15 rows.
  3. Top row is the most expensive aircraft ≤ $200M with at least one valid DXB route.
  4. Exactly one row in the visible set has 🏆.
  5. Change sort to Profit/day → table reorders, 🏆 stays on the same underlying row (it's about total_daily_profit, independent of sort).
  6. Change sort to 'Total daily profit at budget' → 🏆 is now on the top row.
  7. Change sort to 'Profit yield' → top row is highest $/d per $1M.
  8. Click 'A/C $' header → re-sorts to price_desc.
  9. If you have a my_route to a destination DXB serves: that row appears with blue background, 'Currently' column shows the aircraft.
  10. If the suggestion exactly matches an existing my_route (same hub, dest, aircraft): row appears dimmed with '✓ same'.
  11. Toggle 'Hide routes I already operate' → blue/dim rows disappear.
  12. Toggle 'Exclude owned aircraft' → aircraft from my_fleet disappear.
  13. Toggle 'Hide stopover routes' → ⚠ rows disappear.
  14. Lower budget to $5,000,000 → only cheap aircraft, qty_affordable column shows higher numbers.
  15. Budget = $100 → empty state.
  16. Filter that returns >15 results → 'Show all matches' button appears. Click it → all matching rows render, button disappears.

Global /buy-next/global walkthrough:
  17. Click 'Browse globally instead →' → /buy-next/global loads.
  18. Budget = 200000000 → results show mixed hubs, default sort = total_desc.
  19. Hub column visible, contains multiple distinct IATAs.
  20. Same highlight logic, same 🏆 badge.
  21. Click '← Per-hub view' → returns to /buy-next.

Server logs: no 500s, no SQL errors, no warnings about missing indexes.
tasks.testStrategy: All 21 manual steps pass with no console errors or backend exceptions. Visual consistency between per-hub and global pages.

tasks.title: Update CHANGELOG and commit
tasks.description: Record the refactor in CHANGELOG.md and commit the branch.
tasks.details:
Add an entry under [Unreleased]:

    ### Changed
    - **Buy Next**: rewrote as a flat sortable (route × aircraft × seat config) table requiring hub + budget. New columns: qty affordable, total daily profit, profit yield ($/d per $1M), payback days. Six sort options (A/C price asc/desc, profit/day asc/desc, profit yield, total daily profit). Default sort = A/C price desc. Default row limit = 15 with 'Show all' button when truncated.
    - **Buy Next**: existing routes from `my_routes` are highlighted — blue tint when the route exists with a different aircraft, dim with ✓ when the exact route + aircraft is already in your fleet. Toggle to hide them entirely.
    - **Buy Next**: 🏆 badge marks the row with the highest total daily profit at your budget, regardless of current sort.

    ### Added
    - **Buy Next — Global Browse** (`/buy-next/global`): cross-hub variant of Buy Next with a Hub column and a tighter row cap (max 100). Default sort = total daily profit. Linked from the per-hub Buy Next page.

Commit:

    git add dashboard/routes/api_routes.py \
            dashboard/routes/pages.py \
            dashboard/templates/buy_next.html \
            dashboard/templates/buy_next_global.html \
            dashboard/templates/partials/buy_next_results.html \
            dashboard/templates/partials/buy_next_global_results.html \
            CHANGELOG.md
    git commit -m "refactor(buy-next): flat sortable table + global browse + my_routes highlighting"

Do NOT push to origin — leave that to the user.
tasks.testStrategy: `git log -1` shows the commit on the feature branch. `git status` is clean. CHANGELOG.md contains both the Changed and Added entries.

metadata.description: am4-routemine: Buy Next page refinement v2. Refactor the existing per-aircraft card layout into a flat sortable (route × aircraft × seat config) table requiring hub + budget, with three profitability metrics (profit/day, yield, total/day), my_routes highlighting (two-tier: blue for route-with-different-aircraft, dim with checkmark for exact match), 🏆 badge on best-total-profit row, default 15-row limit with 'Show all' button, and a separate /buy-next/global page for cross-hub browsing.
